title: Acute Myocarditis
chief_complaint: Chest pain and shortness of breath for 3 days. I had the flu last week. The pain is worse when I lie down and better when I sit forward. I can't walk up stairs without gasping.

physical_exam.Cardiac: S3 gallop, muffled S1, pansystolic murmur at apex (functional MR). JVD 5cm. Pulsus paradoxus 8mmHg.

hidden_data.ecg: Diffuse ST elevation in all leads except aVR. PR depression. No reciprocal changes. T wave inversion in lateral leads.
hidden_data.echo: Global LV hypokinesis. EF 35%. Mild LV dilation. Moderate functional MR. Small pericardial effusion (no tamponade).
hidden_data.cardiac_mri: Late gadolinium enhancement in subepicardial and mid-myocardial pattern (classic for myocarditis). Edema on T2-weighted imaging. Fulfills Lake Louise criteria.
hidden_data.viral_pcr: Enterovirus PCR positive from nasopharyngeal swab

correct_diagnosis: Acute Lymphocytic Myocarditis (Viral - Enterovirus) with Acute Systolic Heart Failure
key_learning_points: Myocarditis = inflammation of myocardium. Viral (Enterovirus, Coxsackie, Adenovirus, Parvovirus B19, HHV6) most common in developed countries

Presentation mimics STEMI: Chest pain + ST elevation + elevated troponin in young patient with recent viral illness. Differentiate by: Diffuse ST elevation (not territorial), no reciprocal changes, younger age, viral prodrome
Cardiac MRI with Lake Louise criteria is diagnostic gold standard. Endomyocardial biopsy reserved for fulminant or refractory cases
Treatment: SUPPORTIVE. Beta-blockers + ACEi if LV dysfunction. Diuretics for volume overload. Avoid NSAIDs (increase mortality). Antivirals if specific pathogen identified (CMV, HIV)
Most recover completely within weeks-months. Avoid strenuous exercise for 3-6 months (risk of sudden death). 10-20% develop DCM
